codeReview.evidence: Research-only diff reviewed; no production code mutated; JSON evidence validates; limitations documented.

manualQa.evidence: Data, CLI, and adversarial PII surfaces passed for the debtor context graph research artifacts.

surfaceEvidence.invocation: python evidence generation over OCR corpus, route manual, mcp_domain, and Korean-law source map
rg sensitive identifier patterns over .omo/research/debtor-context-graph and .omo/evidence/debtor-context-graph

adversarialCases.scenario: Research outputs generated from real OCR corpus must not expose resident-registration-number, phone, or account-shaped identifiers.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: PII pattern scan returns NO_FINDINGS and classifier raw excerpts are not persisted.

gateReview.evidence: All three ULW criteria pass, focused MCP regression passes, PII scan is clean, and next-build plan is documented.

rerunCommands: for f in .omo/evidence/debtor-context-graph/*.json; do /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m json.tool "$f" >/dev/null; done
rg sensitive identifier patterns over research/evidence outputs
iteration.evidence: Focused tests passed, JSON evidence validates, and PII scan returned NO_FINDINGS.

criteriaCoverage.originalIntent: Analyze OCR corpus, route manual, current TrustGraph/Recova source and MCP surface, plus Korean-law MCP legal requirements before building debtor context graph/domain logic.
criteriaCoverage.desiredOutcome: Evidence-backed recommendation and next-build plan for per-debtor Debtor Context Graph and route/legal ontology work.
criteriaCoverage.userOutcomeReview: SYNTHESIS.md explains what exists, what is missing, why Debtor Context Graph v0 should come first, and how legal source/versioning should be represented.
